commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Codex app-server event projection so failed `commandExecution` items with numeric exit codes other than 126/127 become completed tool results, with focused tests for exit 1 and 127.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +36. Total +51 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main maps Codex `status: "failed"` command items to `lastToolError`, and the proposed diff suppresses that for numeric exits other than 126/127. I did not run a live Telegram or Discord reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer review of the native command failure taxonomy plus contributor real-proof evidence, not a safe automatic repair lane.

target.title: fix(codex): normalize native command exit status
target.body:
## Summary

- Normalize Codex-native shell command exits to OpenClaw exec semantics: a process exit with a numeric code is a completed tool execution, except shell invocation failures `126` and `127`.
- Keep exit code/output in tool results while preventing expected negative checks, such as grep no-match, from becoming `lastToolError` fallback warnings.
- Add focused event-projector coverage for the no-match shape and preserve explicit shell-invocation failure coverage.

Fixes #88332.
Refs #87610.
Refs #85310.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex-native command status projection no longer turns ordinary nonzero process exits into channel-visible OpenClaw failed-tool warnings.

Real environment tested: local source checkout only; no live Telegram or Discord run.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --no-web-search`

Evidence after fix: `git diff --check` passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: Code path now reports `exitCode: 1` command executions as completed tool results and does not set `lastToolError`; exit codes `126` and `127` remain failed native tool errors.

What was not tested: Per maintainer instruction for this machine, I did not run the OpenClaw test suite or live channel E2E because broad test runs can freeze this computer.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --no-web-search`

Not run:

- OpenClaw test suite, per maintainer instruction.
